title: Use Saga with Transactional Outbox for Order Workflow
drivers: Cloud-native fit on Azure Container Apps
context: The order workflow spans order capture, inventory reservation, payment authorization, and fulfillment. Each service owns its own data, so a distributed database transaction is not appropriate. We need reliable event publication and compensation on failure, plus a story for retries that does not double-charge or double-reserve.
decision: Use the Saga pattern coordinated by Azure Durable Functions, with each service publishing events via a transactional outbox and consumers implementing idempotency.

options.name: Distributed two-phase commit
options.rejected_because: Cloud-native services cannot economically participate in 2PC across boundaries.

options.name: Single checkout service (monolith)
options.rejected_because: Conflicts with the architecture's bounded-context discipline and team ownership.

options.name: Saga + Outbox + Idempotent Consumer
cons: Eventual consistency must surface in UX

positive: Workflow state is recoverable from the event log
New consumers (analytics, notifications) can subscribe without changing producers
negative: Compensation must be implemented and tested per step
Eventual consistency must be visible in product copy and UX states
neutral: Observability must include correlation IDs across all workflow steps
Saga state needs an operational dashboard for stuck-saga triage
